content.verdict: Amankora Punakha is the most cinematic of all five Aman lodges in Bhutan — just 8 suites reached on foot via a swaying suspension bridge over the Mo Chhu river. The sub-tropical valley climate, terraced rice fields, and total seclusion make this the most romantic stop on a multi-lodge Aman journey, ideal for the heart of a honeymoon.
content.best_room: All 8 suites are identical in size — book any one and request a river-facing position. Each features a bukhari fireplace, soaking tub, and king bed, but the suspension-bridge approach and rice-paddy views give every suite the same magic.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival via suspension bridge
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive from Thimphu over Dochula Pass to Punakha valley, then walk across the suspension bridge over the Mo Chhu to the lodge. Welcome ara, hot stone bath, fireside dinner with terraced field views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Punakha Dzong
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit Punakha Dzong, the most beautiful fortress in Bhutan, at the confluence of the Mo Chhu and Pho Chhu rivers. The Royal Wedding chapel here is a particularly moving stop for honeymooners. Picnic lunch on the riverbank.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mo Chhu river rafting
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day gentle white-water rafting down the Mo Chhu past terraced fields, prayer flags, and the dzong itself. Afternoon couples massage at the lodge spa with hot stones drawn from the river.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Khamsum Yulley Namgyal Chorten hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Two-hour return hike through rice paddies and a footbridge to this stunning four-story chorten built by the queen. Sunset prayer flag tying at the hilltop. Dinner served on your suite terrace by lantern light.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Chimi Lhakhang and farmhouse lunch
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk through fields to the Chimi Lhakhang, the fertility temple of the Divine Madman — a meaningful stop for newlyweds. Lunch at a traditional farmhouse with red rice, ema datshi, and butter tea.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa and stillness
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow day. Yoga at sunrise in the riverside pavilion. Couples spa treatment, long hot stone bath, picnic by the Mo Chhu. Evening film by the bukhari.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the suite terrace, walk back across the suspension bridge, transfer to Paro airport via Thimphu and Dochula Pass.

caveats: Bhutan visa requires a licensed tour operator — Aman includes this, but plan 4-6 weeks ahead.
Sustainable Development Fee of $200 per person per night applies.
The suspension bridge approach is genuinely scenic but can feel exposed in monsoon (June-August) when the river runs high.
Internet is minimal across Amankora lodges by design — bring a paperback or two.

faqs.question: Is Amankora Punakha good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It's the most romantic Aman in Bhutan — 8 suites, total seclusion, the suspension bridge entrance, and the Chimi Lhakhang fertility temple nearby make it a natural choice for the heart of a honeymoon.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: October-March for warm sub-tropical days and crisp nights, or April-May for spring blossoms. The valley is significantly warmer than Paro or Thimphu year-round.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.answer: With only 8 suites, Punakha is the hardest Amankora to book. Aim for 6 months ahead, especially around festivals and shoulder seasons.

faqs.question: Do we need a guide?
faqs.answer: Yes — Bhutanese law requires a licensed guide and driver for all foreign visitors, included in the Aman package.

faqs.question: What's the best room type?
faqs.answer: All 8 suites are the same. Request a river-facing position for the most romantic outlook.

faqs.question: How do we get to Amankora Punakha?
faqs.answer: Fly into Paro, then drive 3.5 hours via Thimphu and the Dochula Pass. Aman handles all transfers and the final approach is on foot across the suspension bridge.
